title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates a new Codex feature called /goal that allows developers to describe desired outcomes rather than step-by-step instructions. The system enters a "Ralph Loop" that autonomously plans steps, executes them, tests outputs, and self-corrects without user intervention during execution.
writeup: The video demonstrates a new Codex feature called /goal, which lets developers describe a desired outcome instead of writing step-by-step instructions. Once given a goal, the system enters what Jack Roberts calls a "Ralph Loop": it autonomously plans the steps, executes them, tests the outputs, and self-corrects, all without user intervention during execution. The shift matters because it moves coding assistance from prompt-by-prompt direction toward outcome-driven agentic workflows that handle planning and verification internally.
